page_title: kann-ich-bohren.ch: Suitability for borehole heat exchanger drilling
header_title: Swiss Federal Office of Energy SFOE
main_title: Is it possible to drill into the ground and install a borehole heat exchanger where my building is located?
suitability_level_1: It should be possible to drill at your location! Contact your cantonal water protection office to learn more about your canton's regulations, whether there are any depth restrictions, and how you can apply for a permit. Your cantonal energy department can also provide information on subsidy eligibility. Meet with a specialist to determine the best drilling approach for your location. We recommend you get two to three quotes for the installation of a BHE / geothermal heat pump before you make a decision.
suitability_level_2: In many cases, drilling for a borehole heat exchanger is possible. Contact a consultant on heating with renewable energy (or, 'impulse consultant'), or schedule a personal energy consultation. Heating system installation and drilling companies in your area and your cantonal water protection office can also provide you with more information.
suitability_level_3: While it may not be possible to drill at your location, there are other options for heating with renewable energy! You can meet with a consultant on heating with renewable energy (or, 'impulse consultant') to learn more.
suitability_level_4: Unfortunately, we cannot provide this information. Please contact your cantonal office directly.
suitability_level_4_short: Information not unavailable at this location
suitability_level_5: This canton does not provide information for the application. Please contact the cantonal office directly using the information below.
suitability_level_5_short: No information available for this canton
suitability_level_6: The application only works for Swiss cantons.
suitability_level_6_short: The selected coordinates are not in Switzerland
suitability_level_98: The geoservice of the selected canton ({canton}) is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later. This does not indicate an error in the application. You can still get in touch with the competent cantonal office.
suitability_level_99: Please try again or contact us using the contact details at the bottom of the page
info_section_title: Purpose and operation of the application
app_goal: With this application, property owners and consulting professionals can quickly check whether geothermal borehole drilling might be possible at any given location in Switzerland. The application helps to evaluate possible options at an early stage. No legal entitlement can be derived from the map. If a geothermal probe can be drilled, the cantonal map should be consulted to determine which restrictions or requirements may apply. In the case of a construction project, it is necessary to request authorization. Information is provided by the cantonal water protection authority. Depending on the region, the permit application must be submitted to the Canton or the municipality.
map_canton_text: We base our suitability assessments on cantonal data. The data we are working with differs between cantons, and this can make it difficult for us to provide the same information for all locations throughout Switzerland. It may be helpful for you to also consult the cantonal map provided here. We can provide suitability maps for all cantons except St.Gallen, Basel-Stadt, Neuchâtel and Solothurn.
additional_info_4_title: Geothermal Energy: Harnessing Energy from the Ground
hint_1: Geothermal energy is a kind of energy that is stored in the form of heat below the earth's surface. It can be used to heat and in certain cases even cool buildings, for instance by using a borehole heat exchanger (BHE) along with a geothermal heat pump. In Switzerland, you need a permit to drill into the ground to install a BHE. For water protection and other reasons, drilling/operating a BHE may not be permitted at all in certain areas, or only permitted if certain conditions are met. Regulations vary between cantons.
hint_4: You can find more information on the EnergieSchweiz website on heating with renewable energy (available in German, French and Italian).
source_values: Original value(s) from the cantonal geoportal
